title: Share of neonates protected at birth against neonatal tetanus
caption: Between 2000 and 2022, the share of neonates protected at birth against neonatal tetanus through maternal immunization shows varied trajectories across six countries. Thailand, Mexico, Brazil, Vietnam, and India all display lines that rise or remain high, ending the period in the 90% to 100% range; Thailand and Mexico reach the top of the scale by 2022, while Brazil, Vietnam, and India cluster just below. South Africa begins near 70% in 2000, dips to just under 60% around 2005, then climbs steadily to roughly 90% by 2015 before leveling off. The other five lines show fewer sharp drops, with India and Vietnam holding near the mid-80s for much of the 2000s before moving upward after 2010. Across the series, the visible pattern is one of convergence toward high coverage by the early 2020s, with South Africa starting lower and closing the gap over time.
